name: Zerodha ODIN Migration — Strangler Fig
scenario: Zerodha's 15-year-old ODIN monolith needs to be replaced by new Spring Boot microservices without downtime. Implement the Strangler Fig pattern with feature flags, dark launch, and health-gated rollout.

core.description: FeatureFlagService @Service: rolloutPercent (0-100) field, betaMerchants Set. isEnabled(merchantId): beta first, then hash-based (Math.abs(hashCode())%100 < rolloutPercent). setRolloutPercent validates 0-100.
StranglerFigFilter OncePerRequestFilter: for /api/orders, call flags.isEnabled(merchantId). True → forward to new service (set X-Routed-To: new header). False → chain.doFilter (legacy).
DarkLaunchService @Service: execute(operation, legacy, newImpl) — runs both Suppliers, compares String results, logs mismatch, always returns legacy result. Never throws (new service errors caught).
MigrationMonitor @Service: Micrometer counters (orders.legacy, orders.new, migration.mismatch), Gauge for rollout_percent. Methods: recordLegacy(), recordNew(), recordMismatch().
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): rollout=0→legacy, beta→new, dark launch mismatch detected, rollout=100→all new. All 4 pass.
README: Explains why hash-based routing beats random routing (consistency — same user always same service).

bonus.description: MigrationPhaseController: DARK_LAUNCH → BETA_1_PERCENT → ROLLOUT_10 → ROLLOUT_50 → FULLY_MIGRATED enum with rolloutPercent per phase. advance() + rollback() methods.
HealthGatedRolloutService: tryAdvance() only if error rate < 1%. Auto-rollback to 0% if error rate > 2%. Micrometer counter: migration.auto_rollback.
ContractCompatibilityTest: OrderContract record with assertValid() method. Test: legacy and new service both satisfy the contract (same status, amount, merchantId).

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Hash-based routing: Math.abs(merchantId.hashCode()) % 100 < rolloutPercent — deterministic, same merchant always same service. NOT Math.random(). Beta list checked first: if merchant in betaMerchants Set, always return true (regardless of rolloutPercent). Dark launch: ALWAYS return legacy result — new service runs in shadow (user unaffected). chain.doFilter: continue to existing Spring controllers (legacy path) — no code change needed in legacy controllers. rolloutPercent=0 → all traffic legacy; rolloutPercent=100 → all traffic new service.
